# Project Charter

<!-- Generated by `spec-kitty charter generate` -->

Generated: 2026-06-10T19:03:07Z

## Testing Standards

- Vitest is the test runner. The CTS fixture suite is the primary acceptance suite and must pass fully; merge/resolution core logic gets focused unit tests. Every conformance test traces to an RFC-1 section cited in its name or description. No minimum coverage gate.

## Quality Gates

- Before merge - strict TypeScript type-check (tsc) passes, full Vitest suite green including the CTS fixture suite, no implementation code lands before spec, plan, and tasks are locked through the spec-kitty checklist.

## Performance Benchmarks

- Single-soul static check completes in under 5 seconds. Full static fixture suite completes in under 10 seconds. Behavioral suite under 15 minutes against a local 7B model. Static checks require zero network access.

## Branch Strategy

- Main branch: `main`. All missions target `main`; implementation happens in spec-kitty lane worktrees and merges back to `main` after review and acceptance.

## Review Policy

- Solo developer driving an AI pair. Each work package is self-reviewed via spec-kitty review before acceptance; one approval (the project owner) before merge.

## Deployment Constraints

- Linux (Fedora) is the primary developer environment. Static checking must work fully offline. No hardcoded model providers and no credentials in the repository - endpoints are configured at run time.

## Governance Activation

```yaml
mission: software-dev
selected_paradigms: []
selected_directives: []
available_tools: [git, spec-kitty]
template_set: software-dev-default
```

## Policy Summary

- Intent: Ship the reference CTS-1 conformance harness for Soul.md RFC-1 in a ~2-day spec-driven build. Static conformance spine must be complete end-to-end (parse, validate, composition-resolve, report); behavioral conformance is a thin slice limited to three objectively-gradable axes. The fixture set is shaped as an upstream contribution to the unbuilt CTS-1 fixture repository.
- Languages/Frameworks: TypeScript on Node 22 LTS, pnpm, single package. Minimal dependencies (yaml for AST-level parsing, Ajv for JSON Schema Draft 2020-12, commander for CLI, Vitest for tests). No model-provider SDKs - models are reached via plain fetch against OpenAI-compatible endpoints only.
- Testing: Vitest is the test runner. The CTS fixture suite is the primary acceptance suite and must pass fully; merge/resolution core logic gets focused unit tests. Every conformance test traces to an RFC-1 section cited in its name or description. No minimum coverage gate.
- Quality Gates: Before merge - strict TypeScript type-check (tsc) passes, full Vitest suite green including the CTS fixture suite, no implementation code lands before spec, plan, and tasks are locked through the spec-kitty checklist.
- Review Policy: Solo developer driving an AI pair. Each work package is self-reviewed via spec-kitty review before acceptance; one approval (the project owner) before merge.
- Performance Targets: Single-soul static check completes in under 5 seconds. Full static fixture suite completes in under 10 seconds. Behavioral suite under 15 minutes against a local 7B model. Static checks require zero network access.
- Deployment Constraints: Linux (Fedora) is the primary developer environment. Static checking must work fully offline. No hardcoded model providers and no credentials in the repository - endpoints are configured at run time.

## Project Directives

1. Keep specification, plan, tasks, implementation, and review artifacts consistent.
2. No implementation code may be written before spec, plan, and tasks are locked through the spec-kitty checklist. Setup, fixtures research, and spec authoring are exempt.
3. Every implemented conformance behavior must trace to a Soul.md RFC-1 section; tests cite the section (e.g. "§8.1 lists replace") in their name or description. The vendored spec at `.kittify/reference/soul-spec.md` is the single normative source.
4. Locked constraints in the mission spec (C-001..C-010) are not relitigated during plan, tasks, or implement; changing one requires the project owner amending the spec first.
5. No credentials, API keys, or provider endpoints may be committed; behavioral runs read endpoint configuration from the environment or untracked local config.

## Amendment Process

Amendments are proposed by PR and reviewed before adoption.

## Exception Policy

Exceptions must include rationale and expiration criteria.
